name: Reproduce Big Bang Nucleosynthesis light-element abundances
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the observed primordial abundances of light elements - deuterium, helium-3, helium-4, and lithium-7 - given a single value of the baryon-to-photon ratio eta consistent with the CMB-derived value. Specifically: D/H = (2.527 +/- 0.030) x 10^-5; primordial helium mass fraction Y_p = 0.245 +/- 0.003; ^7Li/H ~ (1.6 +/- 0.3) x 10^-10 (with the well-known 'lithium problem' as a mild ~3 sigma anomaly).

regime.description: Universe at temperatures from ~1 MeV (neutron freeze-out) down to ~30 keV (deuterium bottleneck). Time scale ~1 s to ~20 minutes after Big Bang. Energy scale set by the deuterium binding energy (~2.2 MeV) modulo a large entropy factor.

current_bound.experiment: Quasar absorption spectroscopy (D/H), HII region spectroscopy (Y_p)
current_bound.reference: Fields, Olive, Yeh, Young, 'Big-Bang Nucleosynthesis after Planck', JCAP 03, 010 (2020), arXiv:1912.01132

what_it_excludes: Frameworks that modify the expansion rate during BBN (i.e. change effective N_eff by more than ~0.3-0.5), or that introduce extra weak-scale interactions changing neutron-proton freeze-out, are tightly constrained. Combined with the CMB, BBN provides a powerful, independent measurement of the baryon density and is one of the strongest tests of cosmology between recombination and today.
notes: The 'lithium problem' (predicted ^7Li abundance is ~3x higher than observed in metal-poor halo stars) is a longstanding ~3 sigma tension. Some authors include it as an anomaly in its own right; we list it here as a known systematic-or-physics question rather than as a passing benchmark.

parameterization.name: Standard BBN parameterized by baryon-to-photon ratio eta (or equivalently Omega_b h^2) and effective number of relativistic species N_eff. Extensions: modified expansion rate during BBN (parameterized by H_BBN / H_BBN^SM), late-time decaying particles (lifetime tau, abundance), modified neutron-proton freeze-out.
parameterization.reference: Pisanti et al. (PArthENoPE), CPC 178, 956 (2008); Iliadis (NETSurfer); Kawano nuclear network.
parameterization.summary: BBN is solved by integrating a nuclear reaction network through the radiation-dominated epoch from T ~ 1 MeV to T ~ 10 keV. Standard inputs: eta, N_eff, neutron lifetime tau_n, and ~20 nuclear cross sections. Outputs: D/H, ^3He/H, Y_p, ^7Li/H.

procedural.predicted_observable: Y_p ~ 0.247 + 0.015 * (Delta N_eff) for small deviations; D/H is sensitive to eta as D/H ~ 2.5e-5 * (eta / 6.1e-10)^-1.6 with a milder N_eff dependence. Detailed dependences computed numerically by PArthENoPE / PRIMAT / AlterBBN.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Delta N_eff < 0.3-0.5 (depending on D/H or Y_p constraint chosen). eta = (6.14 +/- 0.04) x 10^-10 from BBN alone, in agreement with the CMB-derived value at sub-percent level.
framework_inputs: Effective number of relativistic species during BBN (T ~ 100 keV - 1 MeV).
Modified Hubble rate during BBN (H_BBN / H_BBN^SM as a function of T).
Any new particle decaying or annihilating during BBN: lifetime, abundance, decay products.
Modified neutron-proton freeze-out conditions if the framework alters weak interaction rates.
procedural.notes: Public BBN codes (PArthENoPE 3.0, PRIMAT, AlterBBN) accept these inputs directly. Evaluation against the bound is a single chi^2 calculation against D/H and Y_p once the framework's parameters are fixed.

primary_references.citation: Fields, Olive, Yeh, Young, 'Big-Bang Nucleosynthesis after Planck', JCAP 03, 010 (2020)
Cooke, Pettini, Steidel, 'One Percent Determination of the Primordial Deuterium Abundance', ApJ 855, 102 (2018)
Aver, Olive, Skillman, 'The effects of He I lambda 10830 on helium abundance determinations', JCAP 07, 011 (2015)
